mnemonics.title: Process States Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: NEW TO READY TO RUN THEN WAIT OR TERMINATE: N-R-R-W-T. Remember the cycle: NEW enters system → READY waits in queue → RUNNING executes → (if time slice up) goes back to READY or (if I/O needed) goes to WAITING → TERMINATED when done.

mnemonics.title: PCB Components
mnemonics.content: MEMORIZE PCB WITH: 'PAPA'S PIC I O CAKES' - PID, State, PC, Registers, Memory Info, Open Files, I/O Status, CPU Scheduling Info, Accounting Info, Child Processes, Exit Status.

mnemonics.title: Context Switch Steps
mnemonics.content: SAVE OLD: Save current process PCB → LOAD NEW: Load new process PCB → SWITCH: Update CPU registers → JUMP: Move to new process program counter.

acronyms.usage: Data structure containing all process information - acts as the process's identity card in the OS
Unique number assigned to each process to identify it in the system
The main processor that executes process instructions
Operations involving reading from or writing to external devices
Mechanisms for processes to communicate and synchronize with each other

visualTips.visualization: Think of a RESTAURANT: NEW = Customer just arrives, READY = Customer waiting for a table, RUNNING = Customer eating at table, WAITING = Customer waiting for restroom, TERMINATED = Customer leaves restaurant.

Imagine PCB as a passport containing: photo (process ID), current location (program counter), belongings (registers), home address (memory info), visa stamps (I/O status), and travel history (accounting info).
Think of a relay race: Runner 1 (running process) passes baton (CPU) to Runner 2 (new process). The handoff involves stopping Runner 1, recording their position, and preparing Runner 2 to start.

Family tree: Parent process spawns children. If parent dies first, children become orphans (adopted by init). If child dies before parent reads status, it becomes zombie (waits in 'heaven'/process table).

analogies.analogy: Program is like a RECIPE (written instructions), while Process is like COOKING (actually following the recipe with ingredients and tools). Same recipe can be used by multiple cooks simultaneously.
Like a teacher switching between teaching different classes: saves notes from one class, picks up notes for next class, starts teaching new topic. Time spent switching is not teaching time.
Like a person who has died but their paperwork is still pending at the government office. The person (process) is gone but the record (PID entry) still exists until processed.
Like a SECURITY CAMERA in a building: runs 24/7 in background, no direct interaction with people, always watching/monitoring, started when building opens, runs until closing.
Like a TICKET COUNTER queue: people (processes) waiting for service (CPU). The scheduler decides who gets to the counter next based on the queue order or priority.
